english: Kams źán-pa (khams zhan pa) Mil. of a weak body, of delicate health; also applied to sounds, accent and the like; cf. ṅá-ro (nga ro); ugly opp. to légs-pa (legs pa), v. skye-sgo (skye sgo).

1. bottom of a lake, of a vessel Dzl., Mil.; lower end of a staff Mil.; for under in compounds, as mṅa-źabs (mnga' zhabs) q.v. -- 2. resp. for rkaṅ-pa (rkang pa) foot, mi źig-gi źabs-la _odúd-pa (mi zhig gi zhabs la 'dud pa) Cs., mgó-bos btúg-pa (mgo bos btug pa) S.b., _o byéd-pa ('o byed pa) Cs. to bow down at another person's feet, to touch them with one's head, to kiss them; źabs drúṅ-du (zhabs drung du) c. genit to the feet of ..., for to ..., in directions of letters; źabs rjéṅ-par (zhabs rjeng par) barefooted, e. g. gśégs-pa (gshegs pa) Mil.; źabs _odegs-pa (zhabs 'degs pa) Sch. to help, prob. = źabs-tóg byéd-pa (zhabs tog byed pa) v. below; źabs _oc̆ág-pa (zhabs 'chag pa) = _oc̆ág-pa ('chag pa) II. -- Comp. For the most part they are the same as those of rkaṅ-pá (rkang pa); there are to be mentioned more especially: źabs-kyu (zhabs kyu) 1. spur Cs. (?). 2. n. of the vowel-sign u for u Gram. -- źabs-mgo (zhabs mgo) Tar. point of the foot Schf. -- źabs-sgróg (zhabs sgrog) garter Cs. -- źabs-bcágs (zhabs bcags) Sch., 1. partic. of źabs-_oc̆ag-pa (zhabs 'chag pa). 2. = źabs-c̆ágs (zhabs chags). 3. grounds, territory. -- źabs-c̆ág(s) (zhabs chag(s)), -pýágs (-phyags), resp. shoe, boot. -- źabs-tóg (zhabs tog) 1. service rendered to superiors, esp. to priests, convents etc., by the erection of buildings, or keeping them in repair, or by any aid or work done in their behalf; _otśó-bai źabs-tóg ('tsho ba'i zhabs tog) or źabs-tog (zhabs tog) alone: distribution of victuals, źabs-tóg bzaṅ-po púl (zhabs tog bzang po phul) he placed dainty food before him Mil.; źabs-tóg _o mi brgyál-ba _obul (zhabs tog 'o mi brgyal ba 'bul) we shall provide you with every thing, so that you shall not suffer want Mil.; źabs-tóg byéd-pa (zhabs tog byed pa) a. to render such services b. to feed, treat, provide, offer, Glr. and elsewh. 2. = źabs-tóg-pa (zhabs tog pa) 1. servant, regularly employed in monasteries, by Lamas etc., an official, rgyál-poi sku-gsuṅ-túgs-kyi źabs-tóg (rgyal po'i sku gsung thugs kyi zhabs tog) royal page, Glr. 2. dispenser, benefactor źabs-tóg-ma (zhabs tog ma) fem. -- źabs-rtíṅ (zhabs rting) heel. -- źabs-rtén (zhabs rten) 1. foot-stool Cs. 2. boot Sch. -- źabs-_odrén (zhabs 'dren) shame, disgrace, from mii źabs _odrén-pa (mi'i zhabs 'dren pa) to bring shame upon another, to be a disgrace to him, e.g. a child proving a disgrace to his parents, by a dissolute life, disrespectful deportment etc. Thgy. źabs-rdul (zhabs rdul) dust on one's feet Cs. -- źabs-pád (zhabs pad) lit.: 'a padma below the foot', seems to be an attribute of divine persons, but sometimes nothing more than a high-sounding complimental expression for 'foot'; byín-pa _oe-na-ya _odra źabs-pad _obur (byin pa 'e na ya 'dra zhabs pad 'bur) his leg displays a calf like that of Enaya Pth.; źabs-pad-la (zhabs pad la), Zam. init., seems to stand like źabs druṅ-du (zhabs drung du), so also źabs-pád k̆ri drúṅ-du (zhabs pad khri drung du), in letters; mii źabs-pad stén-pa (mi'i zhabs pad sten pa) Tar., fig. for źabs-tóg byéd-pa (zhabs tog byed pa) to serve; to be a scholar, pupil Schf. -- źabs-pýi (zhabs phyi) servant (male or female), in the widest sense of the word, servant to an individual, as well as a minister of the state or the church, only that the latter service is always referred by an Asiatic to the 'person' of the king or priest; collectively: retinue; occasionally also to be understood as an attending, a waiting on, thus: rjé-yi źabs-pýi _ográn (rje yi zhabs phyi 'gran), we will vie with one another in our attending the lord Glr.; źabs-pýir _obráṅ-ba (zhabs phyir 'brang ba), źabs-_obraṅ-ba (zhabs 'brang ba) or _obriṅ-ba ('bring ba) to follow as a servant, źam-_obriṅ(-pa) (zham 'bring (-pa)) Do., źam-riṅ (zham ring) Cs., źa-_obriṅ (zha 'bring) Sch. servant. -- źabs-bró (zhabs bro), źabs-bró mdzád-pa (zhabs bro mdzad pa) to dance Sch. -- źabs-ma (zhabs ma) drawers, under-petticoat. -- źabs-séṅ (zhabs seng) nail of the toe Sch. -- źabs-bsil (zhabs bsil) water for washing an honoured person's feet. -- źabs-lhám (zhabs lham) = źabs-_oc̆ág (zhabs 'chag).
Sch.: 'the scum left by the evaporation of water' (?); *źám-c̆e* (*zham che*) W. to take off, lbu-ba (lbu ba) the froth, scum; yet cf. rźám-pa (rzham pa).

fem. ma (ma), 1. = yan lag ma tśaṅ (yan lag ma tshang) being not in full possession of one's members, mig-źár (mig zhar) one-eyed, half or totally blind; lag-źár (lag zhar) having only one hand, being lame in one or both hands; so in a similar manner rkaṅ-źár (rkang zhar). -- 2. (= mig-źár (mig zhar)) C., W. blind, rarely in B. -- *źar-té* (*zhar te*) (źar-ltas (zhar ltas)) the winking with one eye C.
Schr. 'following, succeeding', prob. = źór-la (zhor la), q.v.

resp. for k̆a (kha) 1. mouth 2. face, countenance źál-du gsól-ba (zhal du gsol ba), W. *źál-la rág-ce* (*zhal la rag ce*), to eat, to drink; *tśá-big źál-la rag* (*tsha big zhal la rag*) or *źal-rág dzod* (*zhal rag dzod*) please to take some... !
